ontology_gain_requires: non-equivalence to an accepted registry entry
registered held-out predictive, intervention, or compression improvement at matched complexity
documented domain, invariances, estimator, confounds, and failures

variables.definition: Fraction of live cells in a world at a tick.
invariances: cell permutation only when adjacency is ignored
predictive_targets: next-tick alive density under a declared closure model
known_failures: erases spatial arrangement and causal organization

variables.definition: Expected next live density assuming independent Bernoulli neighbors at current observed density.
invariances: spatial arrangement under the independence assumption
variables.valid_domain: homogeneous binary totalistic CA with declared degree and synchronous update

variables.definition: Directed-neighborhood alive-alive frequency minus squared alive density.
known_failures: pair statistic can miss higher-order organization

variables.definition: Observed variance of local neighbor density minus the independent-binomial expectation.
known_failures: not a complete higher-order correlation hierarchy

variables.definition: Sum of absolute normalized axis covariances over the registered finite lag window.
Hamming size and toroidal L1 extent of the difference cone caused by flipping the registered center cell at initialization.
variables.valid_domain: deterministic CA with paired initial conditions
known_failures: one perturbation site and intervention semantics do not establish global causal load-bearingness

variables.definition: Frozen rule-based label over extinction, repeated state, saturation, turnover, and final density.
invariances: state relabeling that preserves live/dead semantics is not allowed
known_failures: does not identify gliders, organisms, computation, emergence, or open-endedness

accepted_ontology_gains.definition: Second-smallest eigenvalue of the normalized graph Laplacian for the connected regular interaction graph.
accepted_ontology_gains.equivalence_class: Invariant under graph isomorphism; not equivalent to coupling or channel dimension in the registered linear predictors.
accepted_ontology_gains.estimator: Exact dense symmetric eigendecomposition of I - A/k for each small regular graph.

valid_domain.simulator: shared nonlinear channel reaction plus neighbor-mean graph CA

admitted_under.role: candidate selection and first untouched holdout

assessment_distribution.perturbation: channel 0 of one node, magnitude 0.5, horizon 80

admitted_under.interpretation: Admission is conditional on this sampling and assessment distribution.

assessment_distribution.perturbation: channel 0 of one node, magnitude 0.5, horizon 80

confirmed_under.interpretation: This confirms predictive utility on a distinct assessment distribution; the larger relative percentage is not evidence of a stronger effect than v1.

accepted_ontology_gains.predictive_target: log10 final paired-state perturbation ratio after 80 synchronous updates following a registered single-node channel-0 perturbation
accepted_ontology_gains.matched_complexity_baseline: Linear model with intercept, coupling, and log2 state dimension; spectral model adds one scalar feature.
intervention_tests: degree-preserving ring, rewired, random-regular, and circulant-skip topology contrasts
fresh degrees, graph sizes, seeds, channel dimensions, and couplings
known_confounds: spectral gap covaries with clustering and path length
known_failures: The preregistered claim that higher-gap alternative topologies recover faster in at least 80% of paired units failed in both v1 and v2.
Prediction does not establish uniqueness, necessity, or mediation.
accepted_ontology_gains.next_falsification: Construct graph pairs that separate spectral gap from clustering and path length, then test frozen recovery predictions on unseen perturbation nodes, channels, and horizons.
